column1: # Merchant Knife — staging rows for merge

column3: description

column1: # Class identity: trade-war specialist, supply-cutter, gilded blade, kingmaker with debts owed.
# === L1 Core fills (5) — defense, mobility, reaction, passive, one more combat ===

column3: Every wound has a counter-entry. As a reaction to taking damage, spend 1 AP to open a ledger against the attacker — they take equal VEIL-weighted pressure damage the next time they act.
You have walked worse alleys with heavier purses. Move without provoking reactions; the first enemy whose space you pass takes a minor EDGE nick as you go by.
Flick a weighted trade-chit into a throat, a lock, or a guard's open palm. Minor EDGE strike that also silences the target's next vocal or signaling action.

Your coat is armored under the silk. Passive: gain a small VEIL bonus to incoming attacks while at least one ally is between you and the attacker, and you cannot be identified as combatant-class on a failed VEIL check.
Open the negotiation from a bad angle. As a reaction when an enemy would crit you, spend 1 AP to convert the crit into a standard hit and draw a card — you remember every blow that lands.

You have been counting since the first blow. Passive: each time an enemy attacks you this encounter, place a tally on them; your next strike against a tallied enemy deals bonus EDGE damage equal to the tallies spent.

You see what a person is worth before they do. Study one enemy; learn their weakest save and highest resistance. Your next VEIL or EDGE check against them this encounter treats that knowledge as leverage.

column1: # === L3 Supply Cutter (4) — sever logistics, starve the enemy ===

column3: You already know which wagon holds the powder. Make an EDGE attack against an enemy; if it hits, they lose access to one equipment or consumable for the rest of the encounter.
Logistics is geography. Mark a zone on the field; enemies who end their turn in that zone cannot be healed or have status effects cleared until your next turn.
Whatever they were saving for later is yours now. Choose an enemy with active buffs or prepared reactions; strip one of them and gain a resource token for yourself.

An army travels on its stomach; a squad travels on its ammunition. For the rest of this encounter, each time an enemy uses a limited-resource ability, they take minor VEIL damage — the route is empty and they notice too late.

column1: # === L3 Gilded Blade (4) — wealthy-class assassin, fashion meets violence ===

column3: The knife is hidden in the lining of a coat that costs more than a guard's yearly wage. Make an EDGE strike; if the target has not yet acted this combat, they cannot react to this attack.
You do not kill in hallways. Move to any point adjacent to an enemy without provoking reactions; bystanders and lower-tier foes do not notice the repositioning.

Raise the glass. Smile. Mark one enemy as the Honored Guest; until they fall, your strikes against them gain advantage and your strikes against anyone else are at a slight disadvantage — it would be rude.
Every guest files past the host. EDGE attack that resolves against up to three adjacent enemies in sequence; the card ends the moment one of them survives the cut.

column1: # === L3 Kingmaker (4) — puts rulers on thrones for debts owed ===

column3: Grant an ally an immediate extra action this turn; they owe you a Debt token. You may spend their Debt later to force a reroll on any check they make.

Choose a minion-tier enemy or neutral NPC in view; until your next turn, they are promoted in your narrative — their actions count as against their own side and allies treat them as a high-priority target.

You tell one ally who the real leader is. That ally's next attack treats the actual target's command structure as the target; commanders, elites, and named enemies take increased damage from that strike.

When a named enemy falls this turn, immediately promote an adjacent enemy into a Debtor — they are now marked as owing you their survival, and any attack they make against you this encounter costs them 1 extra AP.

column1: # === L5 Power (2 more, 1 already exists — the_contract) ===

column3: Call in every favor. Every enemy on the field with an active debt, tally, or mark you placed this encounter is struck simultaneously by VEIL-weighted consequence damage; survivors lose 1 AP next turn.

There is always a cost, and you rarely pay it. Until the start of your next turn, every debuff, damage-over-time, or status effect that would land on you is redirected to the enemy nearest you instead.

You have been cutting their supply lines since before the combat started. For the rest of the encounter, every enemy begins their turn with 1 fewer AP; the first time each enemy uses a consumable or summon, it fails.
A warehouse three streets over goes up in flames. Every enemy in the encounter loses one active buff, one prepared reaction, or one queued ability — you pick which per target.

You set the scene. For the rest of this encounter, your strikes against enemies while you are not the target of any attack this round deal critical damage, and you draw a card each time you kill a named target.

Dessert is served. Make a single EDGE strike with every piece of advantage you have earned this encounter compressed into one cut; if the target dies, all other enemies in the room lose their next reaction — no one wants to be next.

Name one ally the Crowned. Until end of encounter, Crowned ally's attacks cost 1 less AP, and enemies who attack them provoke an EDGE reaction from you at no cost. They will owe you for this — the balance agent tracks the Debt.

Every favor, promotion, and whispered name paid out this encounter returns to your hand as leverage. Every Debt, Debtor, Honored Guest, and Crown you have placed this fight detonates — their bearers suffer VEIL-weighted consequence damage and cannot heal until your next turn.
